app.name: Vaipakam
app.tagline: Lend, borrow, and rent NFTs — directly with other people.

settings.languageHint: Languages without a finished translation show English text for now.

home.title: Vaipakam — P2P lending, borrowing & NFT rental
home.description: Lend, borrow, and rent NFTs directly with other people. Set your own terms — your assets stay in your own on-chain vault, with no pool and no middleman.

borrow.title: Borrow assets — Vaipakam
borrow.description: Lock collateral you own and receive the tokens you need. Direct person-to-person loans on terms you choose, from your own on-chain vault.

lend.title: Earn by lending — Vaipakam
lend.description: Offer your tokens to borrowers and earn interest if they repay. Your assets stay in your own on-chain vault until a borrower accepts your terms.

rent.title: NFT rental — Vaipakam
rent.description: Earn fees from an NFT you own, or get temporary use of one. Ownership never moves — the NFT stays locked in the owner’s vault for the rental term.

offers.title: Offer Book — Vaipakam
offers.description: Browse every open lending and borrowing offer on the network — assets, rates, durations, and collateral terms, live from the chain.

desk.title: Rate Desk — Vaipakam
desk.description: Live person-to-person lending rates by asset pair and duration, executed-rate history, and the signed-offer book.

vpfi.title: VPFI fee discounts — Vaipakam
vpfi.description: Optional: hold VPFI in your vault to reduce protocol fees. Never required to lend, borrow, or rent.

nftVerifier.title: NFT rental verifier — Vaipakam
nftVerifier.description: Check whether an NFT rental listed on Vaipakam is genuine, and see the token’s current rental status straight from the chain.

help.title: Help — Vaipakam
help.description: Plain-language answers about lending, borrowing, NFT rentals, fees, and the risks — plus build and contract info for this deployment.

Unlink removes the Telegram connection.
alerts.linkSignNote: Your wallet will first ask you to sign a short message — it’s free, it’s not a transaction, and it proves this request really comes from you.
alerts.linkIssued: Open our bot and press Start — or send it this code as a message. That connects this wallet to your Telegram.
alerts.testAlertNote: Once you’ve sent the code to the bot, tap this — we’ll push one message to your Telegram to prove it’s connected. Your wallet signs a short, free message first.
alerts.testAlertSent: Test alert sent — check Telegram. Your alerts for this wallet are now on.
alerts.testAlertNotLinked: We couldn’t find your Telegram chat yet. Open the bot and send it the code above, then tap “Send a test alert” again. If the code has expired, tap “Start over” for a fresh one.
alerts.testAlertError: Couldn’t send the test alert just now. Please try again in a moment.
alerts.linked: Telegram linked — alerts for this wallet go to your chat.

alerts.toggleRepayDue: Message me before an interest payment comes due
alerts.toggleRisky: Message me if my loan gets risky
alerts.riskyOffNote: Even switched off, you’ll still be warned right before a loan would be liquidated.
alerts.advancedBands: Risk thresholds (health factor): a message is sent when a loan crosses each level.
alerts.bandsInvalid: The three levels must decrease — warn above alert, alert above critical, and critical above 1.00.

alerts.pushBody: The same alerts are published to Vaipakam’s Push Protocol channel — subscribe there with your wallet and any Push-compatible app delivers them.

glosses.300: Rendered fewer hooks than expected — a hook was skipped by a conditional return.

errorBoundary.title: Something went wrong on this page
errorBoundary.body: The page hit an unexpected error and stopped rendering. Your funds and on-chain positions are unaffected — this is a display-side fault, and a transaction you just signed may still have gone through (check My positions after reloading). Reloading usually clears it.

diagnostics.lede: A quick health check of the connections this app depends on, and a way to report a problem.
diagnostics.rpcFailing: Not responding — the app can’t reach the blockchain right now. Reloading, or switching networks and back, often clears it.
diagnostics.indexerStale: Running behind (last refreshed {{age}} ago) — market lists may lag; your own positions still load directly from the chain.
diagnostics.indexerUnreachable: Unreachable right now — market lists may not load until it recovers. Your own positions still load directly from the chain.
diagnostics.indexerNoCursor: Reachable, but no data has been recorded for this network yet — it will fill as activity arrives.
diagnostics.indexerNotConfigured: Not configured on this build — market lists can’t load here. Your own positions still load directly from the chain.
diagnostics.networkUnsupported: Wallet is on an unsupported network (chain id {{walletChainId}}) — data shown comes from {{readName}} ({{readChainId}}). Switch networks to transact.
diagnostics.noError: No errors recorded in this session.
diagnostics.reportHint: Opens a pre-filled GitHub issue with the details above and the last recorded error. Your full wallet address is never included.

support.lede: Send a message to the team — you get a ticket number back right away.
support.notConfigured: The support inbox isn’t connected in this build, so in-app messages can’t be sent from here. Email works instead:

The team is notified that a ticket arrived (its number and context flags — never your message text or email). Your full wallet address is never included in the health details.

support.mailHint: Prefer email, or want to add more? Write to us quoting the ticket number:

support.rateLimited: A few messages went out just now — wait a minute and try again.
support.unavailable: The support inbox couldn’t take the message right now. Nothing was lost on your side — email us instead:
support.failed: The message didn’t go through. Try again in a moment, or email us:

help.title: Help

assetsHeld.a: In your own Vaipakam Vault — an on-chain account that only your wallet controls. Vaipakam never pools user funds and cannot spend them for you.

missedRepayment.q: What happens if I don’t repay a loan?
missedRepayment.a: After the due date plus a grace period, the lender can receive your locked collateral. If the collateral has a live market price, it can also be sold automatically when its value falls too far — repaying on time avoids both.

lenderInterest.q: Is the interest I see guaranteed when I lend?
lenderInterest.a: No. It is what you earn if the borrower repays on time. If they default, your recovery depends on the collateral they locked — the review screen spells this out before you sign.

nftRental.a: The NFT stays locked in its owner’s vault; the renter gets temporary use rights, never ownership. Rental fees are prepaid, with a small refundable buffer.

vpfi.a: No. VPFI is optional — holding it in your vault can reduce protocol fees on eligible loans. It never reduces network gas, and you never need it to borrow, lend, or rent.

modes.q: What’s the difference between Basic and Advanced mode?
modes.a: Basic keeps the guided Borrow, Lend, and NFT-rental journeys front and centre. Advanced additionally reveals the power surfaces — the Offer Book, the Rate Desk order book, VPFI discounts, and your full activity history. Switch any time from the mode toggle in the navigation; it never moves your positions.

alerts.q: How do I get alerts before a deadline or liquidation?
alerts.a: On the alerts card you can link Telegram (and enable browser push) to be warned as a loan nears its due date or a position’s health drops. Linking is a one-time signature; sending yourself a test alert confirms the channel actually works before you rely on it.

claimCenter.q: What is the Claim Center for?
claimCenter.a: When a loan you’re part of settles — a repayment you’re owed, or collateral from a default — the funds wait for you to claim them. The Claims page lists exactly what’s claimable and for which position, verified against the protocol’s own record, so nothing is stranded.

wrongNetwork.q: It says I’m on the wrong network — what do I do?
wrongNetwork.a: Offers, your vault, and the faucet are all per-network. If your wallet is on a chain Vaipakam isn’t deployed to, a banner offers a one-click switch to a supported network; the app never acts on an unsupported chain.

nftVerifier.q: How do I check a position NFT before buying it off-platform?
nftVerifier.a: The NFT verifier (in the navigation) reads any Vaipakam position NFT straight from the chain and shows its real loan terms and status — so you can confirm what a listing actually represents before you trust a secondary-market sale.

help.disclaimer: Vaipakam is a decentralized, non-custodial protocol. No KYC is required. Users are responsible for their own regulatory compliance.
risks: Borrowing: if you don’t repay by the due date plus the grace period, your locked collateral goes to the lender. Collateral without a live market price transfers in full.
Borrowing: collateral with a live market price can be sold automatically (liquidated) before the due date if its value falls too far.
Lending: interest is earned only if the borrower repays. On default, your recovery is whatever the locked collateral turns out to be worth.
NFT rentals: renters receive temporary use rights, never ownership; the NFT stays in the owner’s vault. Rental fees are prepaid, with a small refundable buffer.
Smart-contract risk: Vaipakam is code on a public network. Bugs, network failures, or extreme market conditions can cause loss — never commit funds you cannot afford to lose.

match.borrowTitle: Lenders ready to fund you now
match.borrowLede: These offers match your asset. Accepting one opens the loan immediately — you take the offer’s full amount and terms.
match.lendLede: These requests match your asset. Funding one opens the loan immediately at the request’s terms.
match.wholeOfferNote: Offers are taken whole — the amount shown is what the loan will be.
match.orPostBorrow: Post my own borrow request instead
match.orPostLend: Post my own lending offer instead
match.unavailable: We couldn’t load matching offers right now. You can still post your own below.
match.borrowerNext: The funds are in your Vaipakam Vault and your collateral is locked. Repay by the due date to get it back — everything is under My positions.
match.lenderNext: Your funds are lent and the borrower’s collateral is locked. Track repayment under My positions; you’ll claim your funds back after they repay.
match.wrongSide: That link points to the other side of the market — a lending offer is accepted from Borrow, a borrow request from Lend.
match.wrongKind: That offer involves an NFT — open it from the NFT Rental page instead.
match.ownOffer: That’s your own offer — you can cancel it from My positions, but you can’t accept it yourself.
match.offerGone: That offer is no longer open. Browse current matches below or post your own.
match.counterpartyBlocked: The other side of this offer can’t transact right now (compliance flag), so it can’t be accepted. Nothing was sent.
match.offerNotFound: We couldn’t find that offer — the link may be old, or the data source is catching up. Browse current offers below.
match.wrongChainLink: That link points to an offer on {{chainName}}. Switch to that network (top of the page), then open the link again — offer numbers repeat across networks, so we won’t guess.
match.termsChanged: This offer’s terms changed since you reviewed it. Nothing was sent — please review the updated offer.
match.illiquidWarning: One side of this deal isn’t priced by the protocol — it has no usable market price or deep-enough trading market right now. If it ends in default, the entire collateral transfers directly to the lender — nobody sells it for a fair market price first, and there is no automatic price-based liquidation. Only proceed if you accept that.
match.interestModeFullTerm: Interest is full-term: the whole term’s interest applies even if the loan is repaid early.
match.interestModeProRata: Interest accrues day by day — repaying early costs less.
match.interestModeProRataLender: Interest accrues day by day — if the borrower repays early, you earn less.
match.linkedLoanAcceptBlocked: This offer is tied to already-running loan #{{loanId}} — accepting it would settle or transfer that loan's position, not start the fresh loan reviewed above. This app can't yet show you the real terms of that kind of deal, so accepting it here is disabled for now.
match.saleVehicleBanner: This is a position sale: you'd be buying the lender side of already-running loan #{{loanId}}, not starting a new loan. The borrower and their repayment obligations don't change — only the lender does. The numbers below come from that loan, live.
match.saleLoanChecking: Reading the running loan behind this listing — the review must show its real numbers before you can sign.
match.saleLoanCheckFailed: We couldn’t read the running loan behind this listing, and the review must show its real numbers — signing stays paused until it succeeds.
match.saleLoanNotActive: The loan behind this listing is no longer active, so this purchase can’t complete. Nothing was sent or approved.
match.saleSellerNotCovered: The seller’s standing settlement approval no longer covers completing this sale, so the purchase would fail on-chain. Nothing was sent or approved — the seller needs to restore their approval (their listing card shows a restore action).
match.saleMaturityPassed: This loan has reached its due date — the position can no longer be bought. Nothing was sent or approved.
match.saleBuyerNext: You’re now the lender of loan #{{loanId}} — the loan keeps running unchanged for the borrower, and their repayment comes to you. Track it under My positions; when they repay you claim the principal and the remaining interest.
match.saleSelfBuy: You are the borrower of this loan, so you can’t also buy its lender side — the protocol rejects that. Repaying or closing early from My positions are your moves instead. Nothing was sent or approved.
match.liquidityChecking: Checking how these assets are priced by the protocol…
match.liquidityCheckFailed: We couldn’t check how these assets are priced, and that check decides an important warning — signing stays paused until it succeeds.
match.linkedLoanCheckFailed: We couldn’t check whether this offer is a position sale of a running loan, and that check decides an important disclosure — signing stays paused until it succeeds.
match.graceChecking: Confirming the repayment grace window this deal is judged against…
match.graceCheckFailed: We couldn’t confirm the repayment grace window, and the review must show the real one — signing stays paused until it succeeds.
match.riskGateBlocked: The protocol’s risk-access rules block this acceptance for your wallet right now — it needs a standing on-chain acknowledgement or access level this app can’t collect yet. Nothing was sent or approved.

borrow.title: Borrow assets
borrow.lede: Tell us what you need and what you can lock as collateral. We’ll show you exactly what you’d owe before anything is signed.
borrow.postedNext: Your collateral is locked while the request is open. Funds arrive only when a lender accepts — we’ll show the loan under My positions.
borrow.collateralWarning: If you do not repay on time, the lender can receive your collateral.
borrow.lockNow: Your collateral is locked when you post this request. You can cancel before a lender accepts to unlock it.

lend.lede: Choose what to lend and the return you want. Lending is choosing a risk, not just a yield — you’ll see the downside before you sign.
lend.postedNext: Your tokens are locked while the offer is open. When a borrower accepts, the loan appears under My positions. You can cancel before acceptance to unlock your tokens.
lend.yieldNotGuaranteed: Expected interest if the borrower repays on time.
lend.defaultOutcome: If the borrower defaults, your recovery depends on their collateral.

rent.durationCap: The protocol currently caps listings at {{max}} — pick a shorter length.
rent.receiptListYouReceive: ~{{fees}} in rental fees for the full {{term}} term — the renter prepays everything up front.
rent.receiptListYouLock: Your NFT {{nft}} moves into your vault and stays there for the whole listing and rental.
rent.receiptRentYouReceive: Use rights of {{nft}} for {{duration}}, starting now.
rent.receiptRentYouLock: {{total}} prepaid — the full term’s fees plus a {{buffer}} refundable buffer.
rent.receiptRentYouCanLose: The {{buffer}} buffer if the rental isn’t closed on time. Your use rights end at expiry either way.
rent.receiptRentWhenEnds: Rights reset automatically after {{duration}}. Close the rental on time from its detail page to get the buffer back.
rent.contractHint: Single NFTs that support ERC-4907 give renters use rights other apps can see; other NFTs still rent, tracked inside Vaipakam.
rent.prepayAssetHint: Renters prepay the whole rental in this token.
rent.paymentAssetCheckFailed: We couldn’t verify the payment asset just now — please retry in a moment.
rent.listYouCanLose: Temporary use of the NFT while it is rented — the renter can never transfer or sell it.
rent.listWhenEnds: When the rental ends, the renter’s rights reset automatically; you claim your fees and reclaim the NFT from the rental’s detail page.
rent.rentFeesNote: The price shown is the rental fee; Vaipakam’s cut comes out of the owner’s earnings, not on top of yours.
rent.title: Rent or lend an NFT
rent.lede: NFT rentals give temporary use rights — never ownership. The NFT stays locked in its owner’s vault for the whole rental.
rent.ownPath: I own an NFT to rent out
rent.ownPathBlurb: Set a daily fee — renters prepay the whole rental up front.
rent.wantPath: I want to rent an NFT
rent.wantPathBlurb: Pay up front, use the NFT until the rental ends.
rent.custodyNote: The NFT never leaves vault custody. The renter receives temporary use rights only — not ownership.
rent.listingPostedNext: Your NFT is held in your vault while the listing is open. When a renter accepts, the rental appears under My positions; cancel the listing any time before that to get the NFT back.
rent.rentalOpenedNext: You now hold the use rights until the rental ends — the NFT itself stays in the owner’s vault. Your prepaid fees cover the whole term; close on time and the buffer comes back to you.
rent.bufferNote: Renters prepay the full term plus a {{pct}} refundable buffer. Close the rental on time and the buffer is returned.
rent.checkNotOwner: The connected wallet doesn’t own this NFT — check the contract address and token id.
rent.browseEmptyCta: Own an NFT? Use the switch above to list it for rent instead — renters prepay the whole rental up front, so an empty market is an opportunity.
rent.browseUnavailable: We couldn’t load rental listings right now. Please try again in a moment.
rent.notDebt: A rental is not a loan: there’s nothing to repay — your fees are prepaid and your rights simply end when the term does.
rent.no4907Warning: Heads up: this NFT collection doesn’t support the on-chain rental standard (ERC-4907). Renting still works — Vaipakam tracks the renter’s use rights in your vault — but apps and games outside Vaipakam won’t recognise the renter.
rent.no4907Unknown: We couldn’t check whether this NFT collection supports the on-chain rental standard (ERC-4907). If it doesn’t, apps outside Vaipakam won’t recognise the renter — renting inside Vaipakam still works.
rent.vpfiPrepayListing: This listing asks to be paid in VPFI, which rentals don’t allow — it can’t be accepted. Please pick a different listing.
rent.vpfiCheckRetry: We couldn’t verify this listing’s payment asset just now — nothing was sent or approved. Please try again in a moment.
rent.vpfiPrepayNotAllowed: VPFI can’t be used as the rental payment asset — pick another token.

preclose.title: Close this loan early
preclose.blurb: Pay everything now and close the loan today — your collateral is released immediately after.
preclose.fullTermNote: This loan uses full-term interest, so closing early still pays the whole term’s interest.
preclose.proRataNote: This loan accrues interest day by day, so closing early pays only what has accrued.
preclose.done: Loan closed early. Your collateral is ready — claim it below or from the Claim Center.
preclose.checking: Checking whether this loan can close early…
preclose.checkFailed: We couldn’t read this loan’s close-early cost right now — retrying. Repaying normally stays available below.
preclose.graceNote: This loan is past its due date. The amount below already includes the late fee for being late (it grows a little each day), and closing this way stays possible only until the grace window ends.
preclose.graceFeeReceiptNote: The late fee for being past due is included in this amount — the same fee a normal repayment would charge.

refinance.receiptYouMayOwe: ~{{payoff}} to pay off this loan, pulled automatically when a lender accepts.
refinance.feesTreasuryNote: The protocol’s {{cut}} cut of the payoff interest settles inside the payoff.
refinance.whenEndsComposed: When a lender accepts your request, when you cancel it, or {{branch}}.
refinance.expiresAfterDays: when it expires {{days}} days after posting
refinance.consentLabel: I understand the payoff and wallet-balance terms below and agree to them.
refinance.receiptReceive: A new loan at your chosen terms the moment a lender accepts — your collateral moves to it automatically and this loan closes in the same transaction.
refinance.receiptLock: Nothing new — your existing collateral carries over to the new loan without ever unlocking.
refinance.title: Refinance this loan
refinance.blurb: Post a request for a new loan on better terms. The moment a lender accepts it, your current loan is paid off and closed automatically in the same transaction — your collateral moves to the new loan without ever unlocking.
refinance.payoffNote: The old loan’s payoff is always principal plus the full remaining term’s interest — even if the loan normally accrues day by day. That is the exiting lender’s fixed entitlement on an early exit.
refinance.walletNote: The payoff is pulled from your wallet automatically at the moment a lender accepts. The new loan’s money arrives in the same transaction, so keep about {{topUp}} spare in your wallet (the interest portion plus the new loan’s initiation fee) while the request is open.
refinance.shortIsSafe: If your wallet is short when a lender tries to accept, the acceptance simply fails — nothing is taken and your current loan continues unchanged.
refinance.periodicWarning: This loan pays interest on a periodic schedule. If a payment period becomes overdue while the request is open, a lender’s acceptance will fail until the period is settled — keep the loan’s payments current.
refinance.done: Refinance request posted. When a lender accepts it, this loan closes automatically — you don’t need to do anything else. You can cancel the request below (cancellation opens a few minutes after posting).
refinance.pending: Refinance request #{{offerId}} is live. When a lender accepts it, this loan closes automatically in the same transaction.
refinance.pendingChecking: Checking the state of refinance request #{{offerId}}…
refinance.pendingExpires: The request expires on {{date}} if nobody accepts it.
refinance.pendingAccepted: Your refinance request was accepted — this loan is being replaced by the new one. Refresh in a moment to see the final state.
refinance.pendingLoanClosed: This loan has since closed another way, so the request can no longer complete — cancel it to also remove its standing payoff approval.
refinance.pendingExpired: This refinance request expired on {{date}} — no lender can accept it any more, and it no longer holds up your other actions here. Cancel it below to also remove its standing payoff approval; the loan continues unchanged.
refinance.cancelSoon: Cancellation opens a few minutes after posting — try again shortly.
refinance.cancelled: Refinance request cancelled and the payoff approval removed — this loan continues unchanged. If you have other listings or requests using the same token, restore their approvals from their cards.
refinance.cancelledRevokeFailed: Refinance request cancelled — this loan continues unchanged. The standing payoff approval couldn’t be removed automatically; you can revoke it from your wallet’s token-approvals view.
refinance.allowanceShort: The payoff approval no longer covers everything this request could pull — a lender’s acceptance could fail (an acceptance after the due date also pulls the late fee and extra interest). Restore it below or cancel the request.
refinance.balanceShort: Your wallet holds less than the ~{{topUp}} spare this request needs — a lender’s acceptance would fail right now. Top up your wallet or cancel the request.
refinance.reapproved: Payoff approval restored — the request can complete again.
refinance.reapproveAborted: This request is no longer completable (accepted, cancelled, or the loan closed) — nothing was approved. Refresh to see the latest state.
refinance.guardrailNote: Your request carries its own expiry and on-chain guardrails: it can only complete at or below the rate you set here, and never after the expiry you see in this review.
refinance.graceNote: This loan is past its due date. A lender can still accept this request until the grace window ends, and the payoff already includes the late fee for being late (it grows a little each day).
refinance.lateFeeDisclosure: If a lender accepts after the loan’s due date, the payoff grows — the late fee for being late plus interest that keeps accruing — by up to ~{{maxGrowth}} more for this request. The approval you grant covers that too, so a late acceptance can’t fail on it.
refinance.expiresAtGraceEnd: when it expires with this loan’s grace window ({{date}}) — a refinance request can’t outlive the loan it replaces
refinance.pendingPastGrace: This loan has passed its due date and grace window, so no lender can accept this request any more — it no longer holds up your other actions here. Cancel it below to also remove its standing payoff approval.
refinance.cadenceChangeNote: Your current loan pays interest on a schedule. The replacement loan will NOT — its interest settles when it closes. Make sure that cash-flow change is what you want.
refinance.multiStepNote: Posting takes up to three wallet confirmations (guardrails, payoff approval, the request itself). If you stop partway, the earlier steps stay in place on-chain until you finish posting or cancel — cancelling also removes the approval.
refinance.partialBlockedByPending: A refinance request is live for this loan. A partial repayment would change the amount and make that request permanently unacceptable — cancel the refinance request first.
refinance.precloseBlockedByPending: A refinance request is live for this loan. Closing early now would strand it — cancel the refinance request first.
refinance.repayWarnPending: A refinance request is still live for this loan. Repaying settles the loan, after which the request can never complete — cancel it from its card afterwards (that also removes its standing payoff approval); until then it just sits until it expires.

approvals.title: Standing token approvals
approvals.blurb: These are the spending permissions your wallet has granted Vaipakam’s contract for tokens your loans and offers touch. Some flows set them on purpose (a refinance request’s payoff, a sale listing’s settlement); anything left over from an abandoned attempt can be removed here.
approvals.revokeWarning: Careful: if a live refinance request or sale listing depends on a token’s approval, revoking it makes that flow fail until you restore it from its card — the card will warn you within moments.
approvals.scopeNote: This list covers tokens seen in your Vaipakam loans and offers on this network. Approvals granted to other apps or for other tokens aren’t shown — your wallet’s own token-approvals view remains the complete picture.
approvals.none: No standing approvals to Vaipakam found for your known tokens.
approvals.unavailable: We couldn’t read your approvals right now — please try again in a moment.
approvals.sourcesUnavailable: We couldn’t load your loans and offers just now, so this list can’t be built completely — rather than show a partial picture, try again in a moment.
approvals.staleNote: We couldn’t refresh this list just now — the rows shown may be slightly stale. Revoking still works.
approvals.revoked: Approval removed for {{symbol}}. If a live request or listing needed it, its card will warn and offer a restore.

nftVerifier.title: NFT verifier
nftVerifier.lede: Vaipakam position NFTs carry real claim rights — whoever holds one controls that side of its loan. Check any token id before trusting it.
nftVerifier.chainNote: Checked on {{chain}}. Token ids repeat across networks — a token that exists here says nothing about other networks.
nftVerifier.checkFailed: We couldn’t check this token right now — please try again in a moment.
nftVerifier.liveTitle: Token #{{id}} is live on this network
nftVerifier.roleLender: Lender side — its holder collects the repayment or recovery.
nftVerifier.roleBorrower: Borrower side — its holder repays and reclaims the collateral.
nftVerifier.roleUnknown: We couldn’t read this token’s role details right now.
nftVerifier.offerValue: #{{offerId}} — this token was minted for an offer that hasn’t become a loan yet.
nftVerifier.lockUnrecognized: Transfer-locked for a reason this app version doesn’t recognise — it can’t be transferred until that flow completes or is cancelled.
nftVerifier.lockUnknown: We couldn’t read whether this token is transfer-locked right now — don’t rely on it being transferable until this reads clean.
nftVerifier.positionRowNote: — holds this loan’s {{role}} rights; verify any position NFT before trusting it.
nftVerifier.lockPrecloseOffset: Locked for a preclose-by-offset — it can’t be transferred until that completes or is cancelled.
nftVerifier.lockSale: Locked for a position sale listing — it can’t be transferred until the sale completes or the listing is cancelled.
nftVerifier.lockPrepayListing: Locked for a collateral listing — it can’t be transferred while that listing stands.
nftVerifier.sanctionsFlagged: The current holder is compliance-flagged: this NFT cannot be transferred in or out of their wallet, and its claims stay frozen until the flag clears. Do not buy this token expecting delivery.
nftVerifier.sanctionsUnknown: We couldn’t check the holder’s compliance status right now — a flagged holder would mean the token can’t be transferred, so re-check before relying on it.
nftVerifier.inKindNote: If the borrower of the linked loan defaults, this claim pays out the loan’s raw collateral in kind — the protocol does not price or sell it for you. Value what you would actually receive before buying this position.
nftVerifier.liveNote: Live means exactly this: the token exists here and its holder controls the linked position. It does not vouch for the loan’s health or the other side’s behaviour.
nftVerifier.goneTitle: Token #{{id}} does not currently exist on this network
nftVerifier.goneBody: Either its claim was completed and the token was retired, or it was never minted here at all — the network doesn’t record which. Treat any offer to sell or transfer this token id as worthless on this network.

keepers.title: Keeper permissions
keepers.blurb: Keepers are third-party services (or your own bot) you can allow to run specific loan actions FOR you — start an early close, finish a refinance you set up, list a position for sale. Everything here is off until you turn it on, and three switches must all agree before a keeper can act: the master switch, the action on that keeper, and a per-loan switch on each loan’s page.
keepers.safetyNote: A keeper can never receive your money — payouts always go to whoever holds the position. You can revoke any keeper (or flip the master switch off) at any time, the protocol can pause all keepers at once, and refinances are additionally bounded by the guardrails you set per loan. If a position changes hands, these permissions apply to the new holder’s settings, not yours.
keepers.masterLabel: Allow my approved keepers to act (master switch — nothing runs while this is off)

initPreclose.label: Start closing a loan early for me
initPreclose.blurb: Begin any early-close path on a loan where you are the borrower. The payoff still comes from your wallet under your standing approvals.

refinance.label: Complete a refinance for me
refinance.blurb: Finish a refinance you set up, bounded by the guardrails (rate ceiling, end date) you approved — and the protocol’s own keeper kill switch.

completeOffset.label: Finish an offset close for me
completeOffset.blurb: Complete a preclose-by-offset once its offer has been accepted.

extend.label: Extend a loan in place for me
extend.blurb: Extend a loan without reopening it — only when BOTH sides have opted into extension limits.

initEarlyWithdraw.label: List my loan position for sale
initEarlyWithdraw.blurb: Start a lender early exit by listing a loan you funded. The proceeds still pay only you.

completeLoanSale.label: Finish a position sale for me
completeLoanSale.blurb: Complete an accepted position sale, moving the loan to its buyer. The payment still routes only to you.

keepers.enabledOn: Keeper access enabled. Keepers can now act where you granted permissions AND the loan’s own switch is on.
keepers.enabledOff: Keeper access disabled — no keeper can act for you anywhere while this stays off.
keepers.added: Keeper approved. Remember: it can only act on loans where you also flip that loan’s keeper switch.
keepers.alreadyListed: That keeper is already approved — edit its permissions above instead.
keepers.revoked: Keeper revoked — it can no longer act for you anywhere.
keepers.maskUnreadable: We couldn’t read this keeper’s current permissions, so editing is disabled (saving now could silently overwrite them). Retry in a moment; revoking still works.
keepers.extraBitsNote: This keeper also holds advanced permissions not shown here — they are preserved unchanged when you save.
keepers.atCap: You’ve reached the maximum of {{max}} approved keepers — revoke one to add another.
keepers.perLoanReminder: Approving a keeper here is not enough by itself: each loan’s page has a per-loan keeper switch that must also be on.
keepers.staleWarning: We couldn’t refresh your keeper settings just now — what’s shown may be slightly stale. Revoking and saving still work.
keepers.unavailable: We couldn’t load your keeper settings right now — please try again in a moment.
keepers.loanBlurb: Your approved keepers can act on this loan only while its switch is on. Actions stay bounded by the permissions you granted in Settings.
keepers.loanEnablesUnavailable: We couldn’t read this loan’s keeper switches right now — the toggles are paused until the read succeeds (their shown state may be stale).
keepers.loanNoKeepers: You haven’t approved any keepers yet — set them up under Settings → Keeper permissions.
keepers.loanMasterOff: Your keeper master switch is off, so nothing can run even with this loan’s switch on — turn it on under Settings → Keeper permissions.

earlyExit.receiptYouReceive: ~{{toSeller}}, paid straight to your wallet in the same transaction — selling to offer #{{offerId}} at {{rate}} yearly. Nothing to claim afterwards.
earlyExit.receiptOweNothing: Nothing — you approve nothing and pay nothing out of pocket.
earlyExit.receiptCanLose: The LARGER of the interest accrued so far or the rate difference for the remaining term — never both. Already reflected in the figure above; the exact amount is re-read live when you confirm.
earlyExit.receiptFees: The protocol’s cut comes out of the forfeited interest — never out of your payout beyond the figure shown.
earlyExit.receiptEnds: Immediately — your position transfers to the buyer and you’re done with this loan. The borrower’s rate and due date don’t change.
earlyExit.title: Exit this loan early
earlyExit.blurb: Sell your side of this loan to another lender with a matching open lending offer. You’re paid immediately from their already-locked funds — nothing to approve, nothing to claim afterwards — and the borrower’s terms don’t change at all.
earlyExit.pickerLead: Open lending offers that can buy you out:
earlyExit.none: No matching lending offers right now. An offer must match this loan’s assets, cover its remaining amount, and fit inside its remaining time — check back later.
earlyExit.unavailable: We couldn’t load matching offers right now — please try again in a moment.
earlyExit.shortfallWarn: This buyer expects a higher rate than your loan pays, and for this candidate that rate difference (not the accrued interest — you pay the larger of the two, never both) is what sets your payout.
earlyExit.forfeitNote: Exiting early forfeits the interest accrued so far: it covers the protocol’s cut and, when the buyer’s rate is higher, helps bridge the difference. The figure shown already accounts for this.
earlyExit.done: Position sold — the payout is already in your wallet, and this loan now belongs to the new lender. Nothing more to do here.
earlyExit.figureMoved: The payout figure moved with time, so the review closed — open it again to confirm against the current number.
earlyExit.moreOffers_zero: {{count}} more matching offers pay less than the ones shown.
earlyExit.moreOffers_one: {{count}} more matching offer pays less than the ones shown.
earlyExit.moreOffers_two: {{count}} more matching offers pay less than the ones shown.
earlyExit.moreOffers_few: {{count}} more matching offers pay less than the ones shown.
earlyExit.moreOffers_many: {{count}} more matching offers pay less than the ones shown.
earlyExit.moreOffers_other: {{count}} more matching offers pay less than the ones shown.
earlyExit.checking: Checking whether this loan can be exited early…
earlyExit.checkFailed: We couldn’t read this loan’s exit details right now — retrying.

loanSale.receiptYouReceive: {{principal}} — the full outstanding amount, paid to your wallet the moment a buyer accepts.
loanSale.receiptYouMayOwe: At acceptance, the settlement is pulled from your wallet: the LARGER of the interest accrued by then or the rate difference for the remaining term — never both. Right now that would be ~{{amount}} {{sym}}.
loanSale.allowanceShortDetail: (a buyer’s acceptance would pull ~{{amount}} {{symbol}} right now)
loanSale.consentLabel: I understand the lock, the settlement pull, and the standing approval below and agree to them.
loanSale.receiptLock: Your lender position NFT, until the sale completes or you cancel the listing. Nothing else.
loanSale.receiptCanLose: If your balance or the standing approval goes short, a buyer’s acceptance simply fails — nothing is taken, but the listing sits unfillable until you restore it or cancel.
loanSale.receiptFees: The protocol’s cut comes out of the settlement figure — nothing beyond it.
loanSale.receiptEnds: When a buyer accepts (everything settles in their transaction) or when you cancel the listing. It does not expire on its own.
loanSale.title: List this position for sale
loanSale.blurb: Set the yearly rate a buyer would earn for the loan’s remaining time and list your position publicly. When a buyer accepts, you receive the full outstanding amount in that same transaction. (If a matching offer above already pays enough, the instant exit is simpler — listing is for naming your own price and waiting.)
loanSale.lockWarning: Listing locks your lender position NFT — it can’t be transferred until the sale completes or you cancel the listing. Your claim rights are unaffected.
loanSale.approvalNote: Listing sets a standing approval of up to {{amount}} — sized to cover settling the sale any time through the loan’s term plus a month’s headroom (the larger of interest accrued by acceptance or the rate difference). Only the actual amount is pulled, in the buyer’s own transaction; if the listing somehow outlives the headroom, the listing card warns and offers to top the approval up.
loanSale.sweetenNote: A rate above the loan’s own rate attracts buyers faster, but the difference for the remaining term comes out of your wallet at completion.
loanSale.done: Position listed. When a buyer accepts, the sale settles automatically — keep the standing approval (and enough balance for the settlement figure) in place until then, or cancel the listing below.
loanSale.pending: Sale listing #{{offerId}} is live and your lender NFT is locked while it stands. When a buyer accepts, you’re paid the outstanding amount and the settlement is pulled in the same transaction.
loanSale.pendingNoId: This position is listed for sale (listed from another device, so cancelling here isn’t available — cancel where it was listed). Your lender NFT is locked while the listing stands.
loanSale.allowanceShort: The standing settlement approval (or your wallet balance) no longer covers what a buyer’s acceptance would pull — the sale would fail right now. Restore it below or cancel the listing.
loanSale.restored: Settlement approval restored — the listing can complete again.
loanSale.cancelled: Listing cancelled — your lender NFT is unlocked and the settlement approval was removed (if you have other listings or requests using the same token, restore their approvals from their cards). The loan continues unchanged.
loanSale.cancelledRevokeFailed: Listing cancelled and your lender NFT unlocked. The standing settlement approval couldn’t be removed automatically — you can revoke it from your wallet’s token-approvals view.
loanSale.cancelSoon: Cancellation opens a few minutes after listing — try again shortly.
loanSale.restoreAborted: This listing is no longer standing (sold, cancelled, or unlocked) — nothing was approved. Refresh to see the latest state.
loanSale.loanSettledWhileListed: This loan has settled, so the sale listing can never complete — cancel it to unlock your lender NFT and remove the standing approval.
loanSale.fundingUnknown: We couldn’t identify this listing’s offer record from this device, so we can’t verify its settlement funding here — manage it from the device that listed it, or keep a generous approval and balance in place.
loanSale.ended: Your sale listing is no longer active. If a buyer accepted it, the payout is already in your wallet and the settlement was pulled via the standing approval; any remaining approval can be revoked from your wallet’s token-approvals view.
loanSale.listingUnavailable: Listing your position at your own price isn’t available yet — the on-chain step it needs is being fixed. The instant exit above works: it sells into a matching open offer right away.
loanSale.partialBlockedByListing: The lender has this position listed for sale at its current outstanding amount. Partial repayment is paused while the listing stands — it would change that amount and mislead a buyer. You can still repay in full or close early at any time.

details.collateralBackAfterRepay: {{collateral}} collateral back — claimable right after repayment settles.
details.collateralBackAfterClose: {{collateral}} collateral back — claimable right after closing.
details.owedPrincipalPlusInterest: {{principal}} + this loan's interest. For full-term loans (the protocol default) the whole term's interest applies even when repaying early; day-by-day loans charge only what has accrued. The exact amount is read live when you confirm; the approval carries small headroom that is never spent.
details.exactAmountNote: The exact amount is read live when you confirm; the approval carries small headroom that is never spent.
details.toppingUp: Topping up your {{symbol}} collateral makes the loan safer and moves liquidation further away.
details.recoveredSummary: What this loan recovered: sale proceeds in {{asset}}, or the {{collateral}} collateral itself, depending on how the default settled.
details.nftRentalBetween: A rental of {{nft}} between two other wallets
details.loanBetween: A loan of {{principal}} between two other wallets
details.addCollateralReceipt: {{amount}} {{symbol}} more collateral, returned with the rest when the loan closes properly.
details.partialOwe: {{amount}} {{symbol}} now, plus the interest accrued so far (pulled together in this payment). The due date doesn’t move.
details.notFound: We couldn’t find this loan right now. It may be new (still indexing) or the link may be old.
details.owedRentalPrepaid: Nothing to repay — rental fees were prepaid (late fees only if closed past the due date).
details.healthReadFailed: We couldn’t read this loan’s health right now — retrying. Liquidation protection still applies on-chain.
details.whatIfNothingRentalRenter: Your use rights end at the due date and the prepaid buffer goes to the owner — close on time to get it back.
details.whatIfNothingRentalOwner: The renter’s rights reset after the due date and grace period; your fees stay claimable here.
details.whatIfNothingRentalViewer: The renter’s use rights end at the due date; the owner’s fees and buffer settle per the rental terms.
details.roleUnverified: We couldn’t verify who currently holds this position, so actions are hidden for now. Please try again in a moment.
details.connectToAct: Connect the wallet that holds this loan’s position to act on it.

done.rentalClosed: Rental closed. Any refundable buffer is ready — claim it from the Claim Center.
done.repaid: Repayment confirmed. Your collateral is ready — claim it below or from the Claim Center.
done.collateralAdded: Collateral added — the loan is safer now.
done.partialRepaid: Partial repayment confirmed — you now owe less.

receipt.bufferBack: Any refundable buffer back — claimable right after closing.
receipt.noCollateralBack: Nothing extra back — this loan has no collateral to release.
receipt.oweRentalPrepaid: Nothing more — fees were prepaid (late fees only if past the due date).
receipt.feesRepay: No extra Vaipakam fee to repay — the protocol’s cut comes out of the lender’s interest.
receipt.feesPreclose: No extra Vaipakam fee to close early — the protocol’s cut comes out of the lender’s interest.
receipt.feesYield: The protocol’s yield fee comes out of the interest before payout.
receipt.feesRental: The protocol’s cut comes out of the rental fees before payout.
receipt.endsRepay: Immediately — the loan settles and your side is released.
receipt.endsPreclose: Immediately — the loan settles today and your collateral is released.
receipt.endsClaim: The claim pays out immediately and this position closes for you.
receipt.owedNoCollateral: Whatever this side is still owed (this loan had no collateral, so there may be nothing).
receipt.internalResidual: Any residual the internal match left for you, plus any VPFI rebate (may be zero).
receipt.liquidationResidual: Anything left after liquidation (may be zero).
receipt.rentalFeesAndNft: Your earned rental fees, plus your NFT back.
receipt.recoveredNoCollateral: Whatever this loan recovered (it had no collateral, so there may be nothing).

addCollateral.title: Add collateral
addCollateral.receiveFallbackCure: A chance to bring the loan back to health — ONLY if this top-up restores the required health level (see the warning above).
addCollateral.receiveSafer: Nothing now — a safer loan (liquidation moves further away).
addCollateral.oweNothingMore: Nothing more — this doesn’t change what you owe.
addCollateral.loseFallback: The added amount joins the collateral at stake — if the top-up doesn’t fully cure, the lender can still claim it all.
addCollateral.loseNormal: The added amount joins the existing collateral — it’s at stake the same way if the loan defaults.
addCollateral.fallbackWarn: This loan is in a failed-liquidation state. Adding collateral only brings it back to Active if the top-up restores the required health level — otherwise the lender can still claim, and the added collateral is at stake too. Repaying in full always cures. If unsure, repay instead.

partial.title: Repay part of the loan
partial.blurb: This loan allows partial repayment. Payments go to interest first, then reduce the amount you owe — the due date never moves.
partial.feesAccrued: The protocol’s cut of the accrued interest settles inside the payment.
partial.endsPrincipalDrops: Your remaining principal drops immediately; interest keeps accruing on the smaller amount.

offerRow.waitingAccept: Offer #{{id}} · waiting for the other side to accept
offerRow.receiptUnlocked: {{locked}} back — unlocked from this offer immediately.
offerRow.receiptLose: Nothing — cancelling an open offer has no penalty.
offerRow.receiptEnds: Immediately — the offer leaves the book and can’t be accepted anymore. Post a new offer any time.

positions.title: My positions
positions.lede: Your loans and rentals, with the one action each needs right now.
positions.emptyBody: Borrow or lend to get started.
positions.unavailable: We couldn’t load your positions right now. Your funds are unaffected — please try again in a moment.
positions.sourcesDegraded: One of this page’s data sources is temporarily unavailable. Your current positions are shown from the remaining source and recently changed items may take a moment to appear.
positions.whatIfNothingBorrower: If you do nothing and the loan passes its due date and the {{grace}}grace period (a short extra window to repay before the lender can take the collateral), the lender can receive your {{collateral}} collateral.
positions.whatIfNothingLender: If the borrower does not repay by the due date plus the {{grace}}grace period (a short extra repayment window), you can claim their collateral.
positions.whatIfNothingViewer: If the borrower does not repay by the due date plus the {{grace}}grace period (a short extra repayment window), the lender can claim the collateral.
positions.graceCountdownBorrower: This loan is past due. Repay within about {{remaining}} — after that the lender can take the collateral.
positions.graceCountdownLender: This loan is past due. If the borrower does not repay within about {{remaining}}, you can claim their collateral.
positions.graceCountdownViewer: This loan is past due. If it isn’t repaid within about {{remaining}}, the lender can claim the collateral.
positions.graceOverViewer: The grace period has ended — the loan can now be marked defaulted, and the collateral goes to the lender.
positions.graceUnknownBorrower: This loan is past due and the grace window may be running out — we couldn’t read the exact deadline right now. Repay as soon as possible, before the lender can take the collateral.
positions.graceUnknownLender: This loan is past due — we couldn’t read the exact grace deadline right now. Once the grace window ends and the default is recorded, the collateral claim appears here.
positions.graceUnknownViewer: This loan is past due. Once its grace window ends, the lender can claim the collateral.
positions.graceOverBorrower: The grace period has ended — the protocol no longer accepts repayment, and the lender can take the collateral at any moment. Once they claim it, the outcome will show here.
positions.graceOverLender: The grace period has ended — this loan can now be marked defaulted. That normally happens automatically shortly after; once the default is recorded, the collateral claim appears here.
positions.fallbackCureBorrower: This loan is past its grace period and a default is being settled. You can still repay in full to cure it and get your collateral back — but only until the lender finalizes the default, which can happen at any moment.
positions.owedRepaid: Nothing — {{principal}} plus interest was repaid in full.
positions.owedDefaulted: Nothing to repay anymore — this loan ended in default and the collateral covers it.
positions.owedDefaultedNoCollateral: Nothing to repay anymore — this loan ended in default and was settled.
positions.owedClosed: Nothing — this loan is closed and settled.
positions.whatNextRepaidBorrower: You repaid this loan. Nothing else can happen to it — claim your collateral back below if you haven’t yet.
positions.whatNextRepaidLender: The borrower repaid. Nothing else can happen to this loan — claim your funds below if you haven’t yet.
positions.whatNextRepaidViewer: The borrower repaid. Nothing else can happen to this loan — any remaining claims belong to the position holders.
positions.whatNextDefaultedBorrower: This loan already ended in default. Any remainder after settlement is claimable (it may be zero).

match.body: These top-of-book offers can cross. Anyone can execute this match and earn the matcher fee — you pay the network gas to execute it.

signed.badgeTooltip: Gasless signed order from the off-chain book — the maker signed it without a transaction, and it fills in a single on-chain transaction by whoever takes it. Signed rows always come from the order-book service, never the chain, until they fill.
signed.partialBadgeTooltip: Partially matched signed order — its remainder still counts as depth and fills through the permissionless matcher, but a direct fill is no longer possible.
signed.rangedBadgeTooltip: Range-sized signed order — its size still counts as depth, but only the permissionless matcher can consume it in slices; there is no whole-order direct fill.
signed.confirmLede: You execute the whole fill in one transaction: your side moves from your wallet, the maker’s side from their vault, and the loan starts immediately.
signed.payCollateral: You lock {{amount}} {{symbol}} as collateral and receive the loan principal.
signed.gone: This signed order is no longer fillable (filled, cancelled, or expired). The book will catch up shortly. Nothing was sent.
signed.makerNotFunded: The maker’s vault doesn’t currently cover this order, so the fill would fail on-chain. Nothing was sent — the maker must top up their vault first.
signed.illiquid: One of this order’s assets isn’t priced by the protocol right now, and this compact confirm can’t walk you through the in-kind default terms that implies. Nothing was sent.
signed.kycBlocked: This deployment’s identity-verification rules don’t cover one side of this order at this value, so the fill would fail on-chain. Nothing was sent or approved.
signed.riskBlocked: This deployment’s risk-access rules block one side of this order right now — the maker or your wallet needs an on-chain access level or standing consent this compact confirm can’t collect. Nothing was sent or approved.
signed.accepted: Signed order filled — the loan is live.

fees.lateFee: Late repayment adds 1% of the outstanding amount after day one, growing 0.5% per day, capped at 5%.
fees.faqAnswer: Vaipakam charges a {{lifPct}} loan initiation fee on the borrowed amount. Vaipakam keeps {{yieldPct}} of the interest you earn. Late repayment adds 1% of the outstanding amount after day one, growing 0.5% per day, capped at 5%. Network gas is separate and goes to the blockchain, not to Vaipakam.
fees.faqAnswerGeneric: Vaipakam charges a loan initiation fee on the amount borrowed and keeps a share of the interest a lender earns. Late repayment adds 1% of the outstanding amount after day one, growing 0.5% per day, capped at 5%. Network gas is separate and goes to the blockchain, not to Vaipakam. Connect your wallet to see the exact current rates.

faucet.lede: Mint mock tokens and a test NFT so you can try borrowing, lending, and renting on a test network — no real value, no cost beyond gas.

faucet.mintedNft: Minted a test rental NFT to your wallet. Its token ID — you’ll need it to list the rental:

liquid.title: Liquid test token (tLIQ)
liquid.blurb: Priced by a test oracle, so it behaves like a liquid asset — health factor, liquidation, and refinancing all work with it. Pair it with mWETH (one as the loan, one as collateral) when a flow needs two different liquid tokens.

liquid2.title: Mock USD Coin ({{symbol}})
liquid2.blurb: A test USDC priced at $1 by a test oracle — a second, distinct liquid token so you can run a deal where both the loan and the collateral are liquid (with a realistic price spread against tLIQ / mWETH) without pairing a token against itself.

mweth.title: Mock wrapped ETH (mWETH)
mweth.blurb: An oracle-priced test token that plays the “wrapped ETH” role in demos. It is NOT real WETH — it mints for free and has no value.

nft2.title: Second rentable test NFT (vART)
nft2.blurb: Another ERC-4907 collection — handy when you want to list one NFT and rent a different one, or run several rentals at once.

faucet.addedToWallet: Asked your wallet to track it.

illiquid.title: Illiquid test token (tILQ)
illiquid.blurb: No price feed, so it behaves like an illiquid asset — both sides must consent, and default transfers the collateral in kind.

illiquid2.title: Second illiquid test token (tILQ2)
illiquid2.blurb: Also unpriced. Pair it with tILQ (one as the loan, one as collateral) to try a deal where NEITHER side has a price — both parties must consent, no health factor applies, and default hands the collateral over in kind.

nft.title: Rental test NFT (vRENT)
nft.blurb: An ERC-4907 rentable NFT for trying the NFT rental flows.

contractError.MinCollateralBelowFloor: The collateral for this loan is below the minimum its size needs. Increase the required collateral or reduce the loan amount, then try again.
contractError.FunctionDisabled: This feature is currently turned off by protocol governance. Please try again later.
contractError.InterestRateAboveCeiling: The interest rate is above the protocol's current ceiling. Lower the rate and try again.
contractError.OfferDurationExceedsCap: The loan duration is longer than the protocol allows. Choose a shorter term.
contractError.AmountMustBePositive: Enter an amount greater than zero.
contractError.AmountMaxMustBePositive: Enter a maximum amount greater than zero.
contractError.CollateralMustBePositive: Enter a collateral amount greater than zero.
contractError.CollateralAmountMaxMustBePositive: Enter a maximum collateral amount greater than zero.
contractError.InvalidAmountRange: The amount range is invalid — the maximum must be at least the minimum.
contractError.InvalidRateRange: The interest-rate range is invalid — the maximum must be at least the minimum.
contractError.InvalidCollateralAmountRange: The collateral range is invalid — the maximum must be at least the minimum.
contractError.SelfTrade: You can't match your own offer — the lender and borrower can't be the same wallet.
contractError.MatchHFTooLow: This would leave the loan below the minimum health factor (1.5). Add collateral or reduce the borrow amount.
contractError.InitLtvAboveTier: This loan would exceed the LTV limit for its risk tier (prices or tier caps have moved). Lower the borrow amount or add collateral, then try again.
contractError.LenderCannotRepayOwnLoan: You can't repay a loan where you are the lender — repaying your own loan isn't allowed.
contractError.PartialRepayNotAllowed: This loan doesn't allow partial repayment — repay the full amount instead.
contractError.RiskAndTermsConsentRequired: Please accept the risk disclosures and terms before continuing.
contractError.PeriodicInterestDisabled: Periodic interest payments are currently disabled on this deployment.
contractError.UnsupportedLoanShape: This combination of assets or terms isn't supported for this action.
contractError.IlliquidAssetNotAcknowledged: One of the assets is illiquid. Tick the illiquid-asset acknowledgement to continue.
contractError.IlliquidPairNotConsented: Both sides must consent to the illiquid-asset terms before this can proceed.
contractError.RiskTierTooLow: Your VPFI risk tier is below what this offer requires.
contractError.FeeTooHigh: The fee for this action exceeds the allowed maximum.
contractError.UserHasNoVault: You don't have a vault yet — it is created on your first deposit or loan. Try the action that opens it, then retry.
contractError.NoVault: This wallet has no vault yet. Open one via a deposit or your first loan, then retry.
contractError.PrincipalNotKYCd: This action requires identity verification (KYC) for this size on this deployment.
contractError.PrincipalSanctioned: This wallet is flagged by the on-chain sanctions oracle, so the protocol can't open a new position for it.
contractError.BorrowerSanctioned: The borrower wallet is flagged by the on-chain sanctions oracle, so this match can't proceed.
contractError.NotBorrowerNftOwner: Only the current borrower-position holder can do this.
contractError.NotLenderNftOwner: Only the current lender-position holder can do this.
contractError.ERC20InsufficientBalance: Insufficient token balance. Your wallet does not hold enough of the token for this amount.
contractError.ERC20InsufficientAllowance: Insufficient token allowance. Approve the token for the required amount and try again.
contractError.ERC20InvalidSender: Invalid sender address for the token transfer.
contractError.ERC20InvalidSpender: Invalid spender address for the token approval.
contractError.ERC20InvalidReceiver: Invalid recipient address for the token transfer.
contractError.ERC721IncorrectOwner: You do not own this NFT.
contractError.ERC721InsufficientApproval: NFT approval missing — approve the NFT first.
contractError.InvalidOfferType: Invalid offer type — duration must be at least 1 day.
contractError.InvalidAmount: Invalid amount — the value must be greater than zero.
contractError.InvalidOffer: Offer not found or no longer valid.
contractError.NotOfferCreator: Only the creator of this offer can perform this action.
contractError.OfferExpired: This offer has expired and can no longer be accepted or matched.
contractError.OfferExpiryInPast: Offer expiry must be a future timestamp. Pick a deadline at least 1 second past now.
contractError.OfferExpiryAboveCap: Offer expiry is too far in the future (1-year horizon cap). Pick an earlier deadline.
contractError.NotCreatorOrNotExpired: Cancel is reserved to the creator while the offer is still live. After expiry anyone can clean it up — the refund goes to the creator regardless of who calls.
contractError.CancelCooldownActive: 5-minute cancel cooldown is in effect for unfilled offers. The countdown on the row shows when the offer becomes cancellable.
contractError.SelfTradeForbidden: Self-trade not allowed — the lender and borrower addresses are the same. Match against a counterparty's offer instead.
contractError.AonRequiresFullFill: This offer is All-or-Nothing — the matcher would only land a partial fill, which violates the offer terms. Match the full size or skip the pair.
contractError.AonRequiresSingleValueAmount: All-or-Nothing offers require single-value amount (min equal to max). Adjust the range so both ends match.
contractError.IocRequiresExpiry: Immediate-or-Cancel offers require an expiry window. Set a deadline before publishing.
contractError.ModifyBelowFilledFloor: Cannot shrink the cap below the portion already filled — partial fills against this offer have committed value at the current size.
contractError.CollateralMutationUnsupportedForShape: Collateral cannot be modified on this offer shape (borrower NFT-rental offers vault prepay, not collateral). Use modifyOffer for amount or rate only.
contractError.LiquidityMismatch: Liquidity mismatch — one of the assets is illiquid and you have not consented to the illiquid-asset path.
contractError.NonLiquidAssetAndNoIlliquidAsset: Both parties must explicitly consent before an illiquid-asset offer can be accepted.
contractError.KYCRequired: KYC required — complete verification for this transaction size before continuing.
contractError.AlreadyRegistered: Country already registered — cannot change after initial set.
contractError.InvalidThresholds: Invalid KYC thresholds (Tier0 must be below Tier1).
contractError.NoPriceFeed: No price feed found for this asset. The token may not be supported on this network.
contractError.NoDexPool: No DEX liquidity pool found for this asset.
contractError.StalePriceData: Price data is stale — the oracle has not updated recently.
contractError.InsufficientLiquidity: Insufficient on-chain liquidity for this asset.
contractError.NonLiquidAsset: One or more assets in this loan are illiquid. Both parties must consent to illiquid terms.
contractError.IlliquidAsset: This asset is illiquid (no price feed or DEX pool).
contractError.LTVExceeded: Loan-to-value ratio too high. Add more collateral or reduce the borrow amount.
contractError.HealthFactorTooLow: Health factor too low. Add collateral to bring it above 1.5.
contractError.HealthFactorCalculationFailed: Health factor calculation failed — please retry.
contractError.HealthFactorNotLow: Health factor is not below the liquidation threshold.
contractError.InvalidLoan: Invalid loan — loan not found or not in expected state.
contractError.InvalidLoanStatus: Loan is not in the required status for this action.
contractError.NotBorrower: Only the borrower can perform this action.
contractError.NotLender: Only the lender can perform this action.
contractError.NotNFTOwner: You must own the position NFT to perform this action.
contractError.RepaymentPastGracePeriod: Repayment window closed — the grace period has expired. The loan must be defaulted.
contractError.InsufficientPrepay: Insufficient prepaid rental balance to cover the accrued fees.
contractError.InsufficientPartialAmount: Partial repayment amount is too small (below minimum threshold).
contractError.NotDailyYet: Daily deduction not yet due — wait until 24 hours have passed.
contractError.NotNFTRental: This action only applies to NFT rental loans.
contractError.PartialRepayWorsensHealthFactor: This partial repayment would lower the loan health factor instead of improving it. A partial repay must not worsen HF — adjust the amount and try again.
contractError.PartialSwapWorsensHealthFactor: This collateral swap-to-repay would lower the loan health factor instead of improving it. Adjust the swap so it does not worsen HF.
contractError.NotDefaultedYet: Loan has not defaulted yet — the grace period has not expired.
contractError.InsufficientProceeds: Liquidation proceeds were insufficient to cover the loan.
contractError.InvalidOfferTerms: Replacement offer terms do not meet lender-favorability requirements.
contractError.InsufficientCollateral: New borrower collateral is insufficient (must be ≥ original).
contractError.OffsetNotLinked: Offset offer is not linked to this loan.
contractError.OffsetOfferNotAccepted: Offset offer has not been accepted yet.
contractError.InvalidRefinanceOffer: Invalid refinance offer — must be a borrower offer for the same asset.
contractError.OfferNotAccepted: The borrower offer has not been accepted yet.
contractError.InvalidSaleOffer: Invalid sale offer — must be a lender offer for the same asset type.
contractError.RateShortfallTooHigh: Interest rate shortfall exceeds allowed limit for this loan sale.
contractError.SaleNotLinked: Sale offer is not linked to this loan.
contractError.SaleOfferNotAccepted: Sale offer has not been accepted yet.
contractError.SaleOfferCollateralMustBeERC20: Sale offer collateral must be ERC20
contractError.AmountTooHigh: Withdrawal amount exceeds the maximum allowed without breaching the health factor.
contractError.KeeperAccessRequired: Keeper access required — you are not authorized to execute on behalf of this party.
contractError.KeeperAlreadyApproved: This keeper is already on your whitelist.
contractError.KeeperNotApproved: This keeper is not on your whitelist.
contractError.KeeperWhitelistFull: Keeper whitelist is full — remove an existing keeper before adding a new one.
contractError.ZeroAmount: No treasury balance to claim for this asset.
contractError.NFTMintFailed: Position NFT mint failed — please retry. If it persists, contact support.
contractError.NFTBurnFailed: Position NFT burn failed — please retry.
contractError.NFTStatusUpdateFailed: NFT status update failed — please retry.
contractError.NFTRenterUpdateFailed: NFT renter update failed — please retry.
contractError.LenderResolutionFailed: Lender vault resolution failed — please retry.
contractError.GetUserVaultFailed: Could not resolve your user vault. Try reconnecting your wallet or contact support.
contractError.NFTAlreadyBurned: This position NFT has already been burned.
contractError.NotAuthorized: Not authorized. This action is restricted to a specific role or caller.
contractError.UnauthorizedCrossFacetCall: This function can only be called internally by the diamond (cross-facet).
contractError.FunctionDoesNotExist: Function not available on this contract deployment (facet may not be cut in).
contractError.AccessControlUnauthorizedAccount: Your account does not have the required role for this action.
contractError.UpdateNotAllowed: This configuration update is not currently allowed.
contractError.EnforcedPause: The protocol is currently paused for maintenance. Please try again later.
contractError.ReentrancyGuardReentrantCall: Transaction reverted due to reentrancy protection. Please retry.
contractError.IllegalTransition: Illegal loan state transition — the loan cannot move to the requested status.
contractError.CrossFacetCallFailed: A cross-facet call inside the transaction failed. Please share the diagnostics export with support.
contractError.SequencerUnhealthy: L2 sequencer is currently considered unhealthy (down or in the 1h post-recovery grace window). Try again shortly.
contractError.SequencerDown: L2 sequencer is currently down. Price reads are paused until the sequencer is healthy again.
contractError.SequencerGracePeriod: L2 sequencer just came back up — prices are gated for 1 hour after recovery. Try again shortly.
contractError.OraclePriceDivergence: Chainlink and the configured secondary oracles (Tellor / API3 / DIA) disagreed beyond the allowed tolerance under the Soft 2-of-N quorum. The price read is rejected so no action runs against an unverified price. Try again shortly.
contractError.InvalidTosVersion: The Terms of Service version you signed for is no longer current. Reload the app, review the updated Terms, and re-sign.
contractError.InvalidTosParams: The submitted Terms-of-Service version or content hash is invalid (governance-side configuration error).
contractError.SanctionedAddress: This wallet (or the offer creator) is flagged by the on-chain sanctions oracle. The protocol cannot pair you with a sanctioned address. If you believe this is a mismatch, contact the sanctions-oracle operator directly — Vaipakam does not maintain its own sanctions list.
contractError.SelfCollateralizedOffer: Self-collateralized offer — the principal asset and collateral asset must be different.
contractError.LoanNotInRepayablePhase: This loan is not in a phase where repayment is accepted right now.
contractError.RentalNotActive: This NFT rental loan is not currently active.
contractError.AssetPaused: This asset is currently paused by governance. Wait for the pause to lift before retrying.
contractError.TradeNotAllowed: Trade between these two countries is not allowed by the current compliance configuration.
contractError.PositionNFTLocked: This position NFT is locked while a strategic flow (preclose / refinance / loan-sale) is mid-flight.
contractError._gasEstimateUnavailable: The wallet could not estimate this transaction, so it would most likely fail if signed. This is NOT a real gas shortage — if the review step flagged a specific reason, that is the actual cause. Otherwise the usual causes are a missing token approval (approve the exact amount and retry) or a stale app build whose call shape no longer matches the deployed contract (hard-reload to load the latest version). If it persists, share the diagnostics export with support.
